Before you start trading, make sure you fully understand the risks involved and take into account your level of experience. Market volatility can lead to significant losses. Only invest funds you can afford to lose.

about.heroSubtitle: A crypto exchange that makes trading digital assets simple and clear
about.historyP1: CryptoX is a student crypto-exchange project built to show how trading digital assets works: buying, selling, staking, P2P and convert.
about.historyP2: Quotes and charts are taken in real time from the public Binance API, and the interface is available in three languages — Russian, English and Kazakh.
about.historyP3: The project keeps evolving with new features and improvements to usability and security.
about.missionText: We believe everyone deserves access to modern financial tools. Our mission is to make cryptocurrency trading simple, secure and accessible to all, regardless of experience or location.
about.value1Desc: Protecting users' funds is our top priority
about.value2Desc: Continuous development and adoption of new technologies
about.value3Desc: Honesty and openness in everything we do
about.value4Desc: We build the platform together with our users
about.joinSubtitle: Create an account and start trading today

prices.title: Cryptocurrency prices
prices.subtitle: Track real-time prices and market capitalization of top cryptocurrencies

legal.doc2Desc: How we collect and protect your data
legal.importantP1: Cryptocurrency trading involves a high level of risk and may not be suitable for all investors. Before you start trading, make sure you fully understand the risks involved.
legal.importantP2: CryptoX is not a licensed financial institution and does not provide investment advice. All trading decisions are made by users independently at their own risk.

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Your security is our priority
security.f1Desc: An extra code at sign-in via the Google Authenticator app
security.f2Desc: Passwords are stored as a strong hash and never kept in plain text
security.f3Desc: Email is confirmed with a code at registration and used for account recovery
security.f4Desc: Without 2FA a $1000 daily withdrawal limit applies — extra protection for your funds
security.f5Desc: All deposits, trades and withdrawals are recorded in your account history
security.f6Desc: Recover access via a one-time code sent to your confirmed email
security.protectionText: On this platform email is confirmed at registration, and enabling two-factor authentication removes the $1000 daily withdrawal limit. Document uploads and KYC are not required.
security.p1: Use a unique and strong password (at least 8 characters, mixed case and a special character)
security.p2: Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) via Google Authenticator
security.p3: Never share your password or 2FA code with anyone
security.p4: Regularly review the operation history in your account
security.p5: Use antivirus software and avoid public Wi-Fi networks
security.p6: Use a confirmed email and don't lose access to it
security.p7: Sign out of your account on shared or public devices
security.p8: Do not click suspicious links in emails
security.ph1: CryptoX will never ask you to share your password or 2FA code
security.ph2: Open the platform only from trusted bookmarks and check the site URL in your browser
security.ph3: Beware of fake apps and copycat websites
security.ph4: Do not click links in suspicious emails or SMS
security.ph5: When in doubt, contact official support by email

verify.title: Account verification & protection
verify.subtitle: Email confirmation and two-factor authentication
verify.b1: A confirmed email for sign-in and account recovery
verify.b2: 2FA — an extra code at sign-in and on operations
verify.b3: With 2FA enabled the daily withdrawal limit is removed
verify.lvl2Req: Connect an authenticator app in settings
verify.enable2faText: Open settings, enable two-factor authentication and scan the QR code in Google Authenticator. After that the $1000 daily withdrawal limit is removed.
verify.faq1Q: Do I need to upload documents or a selfie?
verify.faq1A: No. Email confirmation at registration is enough on this platform. Document uploads and KYC are not required.
verify.faq2Q: How do I remove the daily withdrawal limit?
verify.faq2A: Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) in settings. Without 2FA the withdrawal limit is $1000 per day; with 2FA there is no limit.
verify.faq3A: Sign-in is protected by your password (stored encrypted) and optionally by two-factor authentication. Email confirmation is used for sign-in and account recovery.

bugBounty.title: Responsible disclosure
bugBounty.subtitle: Found a vulnerability? Tell us — we'll review and fix it
bugBounty.whatText: This is CryptoX's responsible disclosure program. If you found a security issue, let us know — we'll investigate and fix it. We're grateful to researchers who help make the platform safer.
bugBounty.look1Text: SQL injections, remote code execution, access to user funds
bugBounty.rule1: ✓ Do not disclose a vulnerability publicly until it is fixed
bugBounty.rule2: ✓ Do not access other users' data
bugBounty.howHeading: Send an email to crypto.x.kilka@gmail.com with a description:
bugBounty.howStep2: 2. How to reproduce it (step by step)
bugBounty.faq1A: We'll review your report, contact you for clarification if needed, and fix the confirmed vulnerability.
bugBounty.faq2A: Yes, we respect your privacy. You can use a pseudonym.
bugBounty.faq3Q: Is this a paid bounty program?
bugBounty.faq3A: No. This is a student project, there are no cash payments for reports. We'll be grateful for your contribution to the platform's security.

glossary.title: Cryptocurrency glossary
glossary.subtitle: A complete reference of terms and concepts in the crypto world
glossary.notFound: No terms found. Try changing your search parameters.
glossary.t1Def: A distributed database that stores transaction information as sequential blocks linked cryptographically.
glossary.t2Def: The first and most popular cryptocurrency, created in 2009 by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto as a decentralized digital currency.
glossary.t3Def: Any cryptocurrency that is not Bitcoin. Examples: Ethereum, Ripple, Litecoin.
glossary.t4Def: A software or hardware device for storing private keys and managing cryptocurrency assets.
glossary.t5Def: A secret cryptographic code that gives access to your crypto assets. Losing the private key means losing access to funds.
glossary.t6Def: A cryptographic address that can be safely shared with others to receive payments.
glossary.t7Def: The process of confirming transactions and adding new blocks to the blockchain using computing power. Miners receive rewards in cryptocurrency.
glossary.t8Def: The process of locking cryptocurrency to support the blockchain network and earn rewards.
glossary.t9Def: A self-executing contract whose terms are written in blockchain code and automatically executed when conditions are met.
glossary.t10Def: Decentralized finance - blockchain-based financial services without traditional intermediaries.
glossary.t11Def: Non-fungible token - a unique digital asset representing ownership of a specific item or content.
glossary.t12Def: Fear Of Missing Out - a psychological effect that pushes investors to buy at the peak price.
glossary.t13Def: Fear, Uncertainty and Doubt - spreading negative information to manipulate the market.
glossary.t14Def: A long-term cryptocurrency holding strategy regardless of price fluctuations. Originates from a typo of the word 'hold'.
glossary.t15Def: A platform for buying, selling and exchanging cryptocurrencies.
glossary.t16Def: The total market value of a cryptocurrency, calculated as the price of one coin multiplied by the number of coins in circulation.
glossary.t17Def: The degree of price variability of an asset. Cryptocurrencies are known for high volatility.
glossary.t18Def: Storing cryptocurrency in an offline wallet not connected to the internet for maximum security.
glossary.t19Def: A crypto wallet connected to the internet, convenient for frequent transactions but less secure.
glossary.t20Def: A fee for executing transactions on the Ethereum blockchain and other networks.
glossary.t21Def: A consensus algorithm that requires solving complex mathematical problems to confirm transactions.
glossary.t22Def: A consensus algorithm where validators are selected based on the number of coins held.
glossary.t23Def: A manipulative scheme of artificially inflating an asset's price followed by mass selling.
glossary.t24Def: A technical document describing the concept, technology and roadmap of a crypto project.

support.title: Help center
support.subtitle: Find answers to frequently asked questions
support.q1: How do I create an account on CryptoX?
support.a1: Click 'Sign up', enter your name, email and password, then confirm your email with the code from the letter. Your account is then ready to use.
support.q2: How do I top up my balance?
support.a2: Go to 'Wallet' → 'Deposit', enter the amount and your bank card details. The balance is topped up in USD with no fee.
support.a3: Deposits are free, trading is 1% per trade, and withdrawals are 1% of the amount. See the 'Fees' page for details.
support.q4: How long does a withdrawal take?
support.a4: Withdrawals to a bank card take 1–3 business days.
support.a5: 2FA is an extra layer of protection. After your password, a code from the Google Authenticator app is required. We recommend enabling 2FA in settings.
support.a6: No. Email confirmation at registration is enough. To remove the $1000 daily withdrawal limit, enable 2FA in settings.
support.q7: What if I forgot my password?
support.a7: Click 'Forgot password?' on the login page and enter your email — we'll send a code to reset your password.
support.a8: Yes. You can buy and sell cryptocurrency directly with other users in the P2P section.
support.a9: Open 'Wallet' → 'Withdraw', enter the amount and your bank card number. Minimum $10, 1% fee, credited in 1–3 business days.
support.q10: Which documents are needed for verification?
support.a10: No documents are required — verification is limited to email confirmation at registration.
support.a11: Open settings, enable two-factor authentication, scan the QR code in Google Authenticator and enter the confirmation code.
support.q12: Is the fee the same for buying and selling?
support.a12: Yes, a single 1% fee applies to every trade — buying, selling and converting.
support.q13: How do I buy or sell cryptocurrency?
support.a13: Open the 'Market' section, choose a coin, click 'Buy' or 'Sell' and enter the amount. The trade is executed at the current market price.
support.a14: Without 2FA it is $1000 per day. After enabling 2FA the daily limit is removed.
support.a15: Yes. If you forgot your password, use 'Forgot password?' on the login page — a reset code will be sent to your email.
support.q16: Is there a minimum trade amount?
support.a16: You can buy cryptocurrency for any amount within your balance at the current price.
support.q17: How do I protect my account from hacking?
support.a17: Enable 2FA, use a strong unique password, never share your 2FA code, check your operation history and don't click suspicious links.
support.a18: No, topping up your balance by bank card is free. The 1% fee applies only to trading and withdrawals.
support.q19: What currency is the balance kept in?
support.a19: The wallet balance is kept in US dollars (USD). The value of crypto assets is recalculated using current Binance prices.
support.q20: How does staking work on CryptoX?
support.a20: Open 'Staking', choose a coin and a plan, and enter the amount. Rewards are credited according to the selected plan.
support.notFound: No questions found. Try changing your search parameters.
support.ctaTitle: Didn't find an answer to your question?
support.ctaSubtitle: Send us an email — we'll help.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.noticeText: By using the CryptoX platform, you agree to these terms of use. If you do not agree with any provisions, please do not use our services.
terms.s1p1: This User Agreement (the "Agreement") governs the use of the CryptoX platform and all related services.
terms.s1p2: CryptoX (the "Platform", "we", "us") provides an online service for trading cryptocurrencies and digital assets.
terms.s1p3: The Agreement takes effect upon registration on the platform or the start of using any of our services.
terms.s2i1: You must be at least 18 years old to use the platform
terms.s2i2: You must provide accurate and up-to-date information during registration
terms.s2i3: You are responsible for keeping your password and login data safe
terms.s2i4: Creating more than one account per user is prohibited
terms.s2i5: We reserve the right to refuse registration without explanation
terms.s3p1: At registration we confirm your email address. You can additionally enable two-factor authentication (2FA) to protect your account.
terms.s3i1: • Email confirmation via a code from the letter
terms.s3i3: • Document uploads and KYC are not required
terms.s3p3: Without a confirmed email, access to some features is limited.
terms.s4p1: Trading on the platform is carried out under the conditions specified on the website:
terms.s4i4: We reserve the right to change fees with prior notice
terms.s5p1: When using the platform, the following is strictly prohibited:
terms.s5i2: Fraud and use of other people's documents
terms.s5i4: Using bots and automated systems without permission
terms.s5i5: Violation of the laws of any jurisdiction
terms.s6p1: Trading cryptocurrencies involves high risks, including:
terms.s6p2: The Company is not responsible for your trading decisions and their results. We do not provide investment advice.
terms.s7p1: We reserve the right to change this Agreement at any time. All changes take effect upon publication on the website. By continuing to use the platform after changes are made, you agree to the new terms.
terms.s8p1: For questions related to the terms of use, contact:

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.introP1: CryptoX ("we", "us", "our") takes the protection of your privacy seriously. This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, store and protect your personal information.
privacy.introP2: By using our platform, you agree to the terms of this Privacy Policy.
privacy.s1c2i3: • History of your operations on the platform
privacy.s2i1Text: — to sign in to your account and protect it
privacy.s2i4Text: — to send emails (email confirmation, password reset) and respond to your requests
privacy.s2i5Text: — to analyze and optimize the platform
privacy.s3intro: We apply modern security measures to protect your data:
privacy.s3a1Desc: SSL/TLS encryption for all transmitted data
privacy.s3a2Desc: An extra layer of account protection
privacy.s3a3Desc: Passwords are stored as an irreversible hash
privacy.s3a4Desc: Access is protected by tokens; sessions can be ended
privacy.s4intro: We may share your information with the following third parties:
privacy.s4i2Text: — to send emails (email confirmation, password reset)
privacy.s4i3Text: — to host the website and database
privacy.s4notePost: your personal data to third parties for marketing purposes.
privacy.s5intro: In accordance with data protection law, you have the right to:
privacy.s5i1Text: — request a copy of your personal data
privacy.s5i3Text: — request deletion of your data (subject to conditions)
privacy.s5i4Text: — restrict the use of your data
privacy.s5i5Text: — receive data in a structured format
privacy.s5contactPre: To exercise your rights, contact us at:
privacy.s6p1: We store your data for as long as necessary to operate the service and fulfill the purposes specified in this Policy. After you delete your account, the data associated with it is deleted.
privacy.s7pre: We use cookies to improve the platform. Read more in our

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro1p1: Cookies are small text files saved on your device (computer, smartphone or tablet) when you visit websites. They help websites remember your actions and preferences, providing a more convenient experience.
cookies.intro1p2: CryptoX uses cookies to improve the platform, ensure security and provide a personalized experience.
cookies.typesTitle: What types of cookies we use
cookies.type1Desc: These cookies are essential for the site to work and cannot be disabled.
cookies.type2Desc: These cookies improve functionality and personalization.
cookies.browserDesc: You can control and delete cookies through your browser settings:
cookies.firefoxText: Settings → Privacy & Security → Cookies and Site Data
cookies.warnText: Disabling necessary cookies may break the platform. Some features may become unavailable.
cookies.thirdIntro: Some external services we use may set technical cookies:
cookies.third1Text: — to send emails (email confirmation, password reset)
cookies.third2Text: — to host the website and API
cookies.thirdNote: We do not connect third-party analytics or advertising networks.
cookies.updatesP1: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. All changes will be published on this page. We recommend periodically checking this page for up-to-date information.
cookies.questionsP1: If you have questions about our use of cookies, contact us:
